back_translation: For the choirmaster. A psalm of the sons of Korah.
Hear this, all you peoples; listen, all inhabitants of the world,
both low and high, rich and poor alike.
My mouth will speak words of wisdom, and the meditation of my heart will bring understanding.
I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will unfold my riddle with the harp.
Why should I fear in times of trouble, when the iniquity of those who would supplant me surrounds me—
those who trust in their wealth and boast in their great riches?
No one can by any means redeem his brother, or give to God a ransom for him—
for the redemption of their soul is costly, and can never suffice—
that he should live on forever and not see decay.
For it is evident that wise men die; the fool and the senseless alike perish and leave their wealth to others.
Their inward thought is that their houses will last forever, their dwellings for all generations; so they call their lands by their own names.
But man, despite his wealth, does not endure; he is like the beasts that perish.
This is the way of those who are foolish, and of their followers who approve their sayings. Selah
Like sheep they are appointed for Sheol; death will shepherd them. The upright will rule over them in the morning; their form will decay in Sheol, far from their lofty dwelling.
But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol, for He will take me. Selah
Do not be afraid when a man grows rich, when the splendor of his house increases,
for when he dies he will take nothing away; his wealth will not go down after him.
Though in his lifetime he counts himself blessed—and men praise you when you do well for yourself—
he will go to the generation of his fathers, who will never again see the light.
A man who has wealth but no understanding is like the beasts that perish.
